from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # simple verb mapping for readability
    verb_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has an elevation of",
        "location": "is located at",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of",
        "runwayName": "has runway named",
        "country": "is in",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has a first runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway length of",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "has elevation of",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code",
        "nativeName": "has native name",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to party",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has first runway number",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "has elevation of",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in administrative arrondissement",
        "mayor": "has mayor",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has second runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has third runway surface of",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has runway surface of",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language",
        "city": "is city of",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "headquarter": "has headquarter at",
        "class": "belongs to class",
        "division": "belongs to division",
        "order": "belongs to order",
        "regionServed": "serves region",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport",
        "aircraftFighter": "operates fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": "operates attack aircraft",
        "battle": "participated in battle",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has fifth runway surface of",
        "countySeat": "has county seat",
        "chief": "has chief",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "areaCode": "has area code",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in ceremonial county",
    }
    # Group predicates by subject to build a single coherent sentence per subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    # Helper to clean quoted strings and add appropriate units
    def format_object(predicate: str, obj: str) -> str:
        # Strip surrounding quotes if present
        if obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        # Add units for length/elevation predicates
        lower = predicate.lower()
        if "feet" in lower:
            # avoid double‑adding if unit already present
            if not obj.lower().endswith("feet"):
                obj = f"{obj} feet"
        elif "metre" in lower or "metres" in lower:
            if not obj.lower().endswith("metres"):
                obj = f"{obj} metres"
        return obj

    subject_phrases = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, "has")
        obj_formatted = format_object(t.predicate, t.object)
        subject_phrases[t.subject].append(f"{verb} {obj_formatted}")

    # Helper to join a list of phrases into a natural‑language list
    def join_phrases(phrases: list[str]) -> str:
        if not phrases:
            return ""
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            return phrases[0]
        if len(phrases) == 2:
            return f"{phrases[0]} and {phrases[1]}"
        # three or more
        return ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + f", and {phrases[-1]}"

    # Build a clause per subject
    subject_clauses = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        if not phrases:
            continue
        clause_body = join_phrases(phrases)
        subject_clauses.append(f"{subj} {clause_body}")

    # Join all subject clauses into a single complex sentence
    full_sentence = join_phrases(subject_clauses)
    return full_sentence + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence.
